title: Python + Gen AI Engineer - Consultant
description:
About KPMG in India

KPMG entities in India are professional services firm(s).
These Indian member firms are affiliated with KPMG
International Limited. KPMG was established in India in
August 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network
of firms, and are conversant with local laws, regulations,
markets and competition. KPMG has offices across India in
Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Chandigarh, Chennai, Gurugram,
Hyderabad, Jaipur, Kochi, Kolkata, Mumbai, Noida, Pune,
Vadodara and Vijayawada.

KPMG entities in India offer services to national and
international clients in India across sectors. We strive to
provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focussed and
technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared
knowledge of global and local industries and our experience
of the Indian business environment.

The person will work on a variety of projects in a highly
collaborative, fast-paced environment. The person will be
responsible for software development activities of KPMG,
India. Part of the development team, he/she will work on the
full life cycle of the process, develop code and unit
testing. He/she will work closely with Technical Architect,
Business Analyst, user interaction designers, and other
software engineers to develop new product offerings and
improve existing ones. Additionally, the person will ensure
that all development practices are in compliance with KPMG’s
best practices policies and procedures. This role requires
quick ramp up on new technologies whenever required.

Responsibilities

Role : Python + Gen AI Engineer

Location: Mumbai

Experience: 4 to 6 years

Technical Skills

- Strong proficiency in Python (OOP, APIs, async programming).
- Hands-on experience with LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and prompt
  engineering.
- Experience with libraries such as:
  - LangChain, LlamaIndex
  - Transformers (HuggingFace)
  - PyTorch / TensorFlow
- Experience with vector stores and RAG pipeline design.
- Knowledge of cloud AI environments (Azure OpenAI, AWS
  Bedrock, GCP Vertex AI).

Nice-to-Have

- Experience fine-tuning large models (instruction-tuning,
  LoRA, QLoRA).
- Experience with MLOps tools (MLflow, Weights & Biases).
- Understanding of distributed systems and scalability
  patterns.
- Exposure to multimodal models (Vision+Language, Speech,
  etc.)

Qualifications

Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science,
Information Technology, or a related field.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

KPMG India has a policy of providing equal opportunity for
all applicants and employees regardless of their color,
caste, religion, age, sex/gender, national origin,
citizenship, sexual orientation, gender identity or
expression, disability or other legally protected status.
KPMG India values diversity and we request you to submit the
details below to support us in our endeavor for diversity.
Providing the below information is voluntary and refusal to
submit such information will not be prejudicial to you.
responsibilities: Develop AI-powered software solutions using Python and GenAI technologies
Design and implement RAG pipelines and vector store integrations
Work with LLMs and GenAI frameworks including LangChain and LlamaIndex
Implement prompt engineering strategies and model fine-tuning when required
Collaborate with Technical Architects, Business Analysts, and other engineers on product development
Ensure development practices comply with KPMG best practices
Deploy AI solutions on cloud platforms (Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, GCP Vertex AI)
Quickly ramp up on new technologies as needed
use_cases: Enterprise AI solutions for KPMG clients
RAG-based information retrieval and knowledge systems
Custom AI application development for consulting engagements
AI-powered automation and decision support systems
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves hands-on work with LLMs, GenAI frameworks
(LangChain, LlamaIndex), RAG pipeline design, and prompt
engineering. The engineer will build and deploy AI systems
using cloud AI services, with nice-to-have experience in
fine-tuning models using LoRA/QLoRA techniques.
